name: AbuseIPDB
description: This app integrates with AbuseIPDB to perform investigative actions
setup_instructions:
## Get an AbuseIPDB API key

AbuseIPDB requires a free account; the **Free** tier allows 1,000 checks/day, **Webmaster** 3,000/day, paid plans go higher.

### 1. Register an account
1. Open https://www.abuseipdb.com/register and create an account (email + password). Verify the confirmation email.

### 2. Create an API key
1. After logging in, go to https://www.abuseipdb.com/account/api.
2. Click **Create Key**, give it a label (e.g. `T1 Agentics`), and click **Create**.
3. Copy the key string. AbuseIPDB only shows it once on creation — if you lose it, you'll need to make a new one.

### 3. Paste the key below
Paste in the **API Key** field. T1 sends it as the `Key` request header automatically (and adds the required `Accept: application/json`).

Click **Test Authentication** to confirm. The test calls `/check?ipAddress=1.1.1.1` and expects a 200.

### Rate limits
- **Free**: 1,000 IP checks/day, 100 reports/day.
- **Webmaster** (free, requires verifying domain ownership): 3,000 checks/day.
- **Premium**: 10K–100K+ checks/day depending on plan; also unlocks bulk endpoints.

T1 surfaces a `QuotaExceededError` on 429.

### Troubleshooting
- **`401 Unauthorized` / `Authentication failed`**: Key was deleted or mistyped. Generate a new one in the API page.
- **`429 Too Many Requests`**: Daily quota hit. Resets at 00:00 UTC.
- **`422 Unprocessable Entity`** on report: missing required `categories` parameter — it must be a comma-separated list of integer category IDs (see https://www.abuseipdb.com/categories).

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Check for IP Reports within this number of days

actions.name: Post Ip
actions.description: Report an IP for abusive behavior

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: IPv4 or IPv6 to report on

parameters.name: category_ids
parameters.description: Comma delineated list of category IDs
